If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Issue reported by:** @sakluger **Slack conversation**: [#migrate](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C07NMDKEFMH/p1773159772643849) ## Action Performed: 1. Import a new personal card in OldDot or NewDot. 2. Go to one of the expenses in NewDot. ## Expected Result: You should see the toggle for whether it's reimbursable, just like in classic. It should default to the card-level selection in effect at the time the transaction was imported. This setting for personal card expenses is not affected by the workspace rule that determines whether cash expenses are set to be reimbursable. ## Actual Result: There's no reimbursable toggle for personal card expenses ## Workaround: This can be changed in OldDot ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: Report shows different expenses in Classic vs New Expensify — a corporate card expense attached to the report is visible in Classic but missing in New (when required tags/coding are incomplete).

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel!
___

**Version Number:** 
**Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Reproduction blocked)
**Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction
**If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:**
**Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):**
**Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856
**Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:**
**Issue reported by:** @Leeckhart
**Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773153785305069)

## Action Performed:
1. Open the same expense report in Classic Expensify
2. Locate the merchant (xyz) within the report
3. Observe two (xyz) expenses are present on the report: One cash expense, One corporate card (Chase) expense
4. Open the corporate card  expense in Classic and note it is missing required coding (e.g., Locations/Departments)
5. Open the same report in New Expensify
6. Locate the merchant (xyz)
7. Observe only one  expense appears (the cash expense). The corporate card expense that is visible/attached in Classic is not shown in NewDot.
## Expected Result:
New Expensify should display the same set of expenses attached to the report as Classic. If an expense is missing required tags (e.g., Location), it should still be visible on the report with validation errors/warnings, not disappear from the report view.
## Actual Result:
In New Expensify, the corporate card expense attached to the report (visible in Classic) does not appear on the report at all. Only the cash expense is shown in NewDot. This creates a mismatch between Classic and New report contents. They are getting an export error related to the missing tag, but they can't see that on New Expensify, they shouldn't have to go back to Classic to check things if they were migrated.
## Workaround:
Unknown
## Platforms:
Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced:
- [ ] Android: App
- [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome
- [ ] iOS: App
- [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari
- [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome
- [x] Windows: Chrome
- [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari

## Screenshots/Videos

The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1773153785305069)

[View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

Go to staging.new.expensify.com 2. Go to workspace settings > Members. 3. Click on your profile. → Assign cards section is shown on member profile RHP. 4. Go to workspace settings > Company cards. 5. Click Settings > Remove card feed. 6. Click Delete. 7. Go to workspace settings > Members. 8. Click on your profile. ## Expected Result: The assigned Expensify card will appear on member profile RHP when direct feed is deleted. ## Actual Result: The assigned Expensify card disappears from member profile RHP when direct feed is deleted. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [x] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [x] iOS: mWeb Safari - [x] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4b0edf71-2997-42a9-8f68-1f51c5be3dd3 [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)
